core_mechanism: Schmidt (singular-value) decomposition of a bipartitioned composite state, recast as a rank-ordered spectrum of levels whose gap-separated low-lying part serves as a robust invariant/fingerprint for classification
brief_reason: The abstract defines the 'entanglement spectrum' as a presentation of the Schmidt decomposition treated like energy levels, and proposes that its low-lying levels, split off by an 'entanglement gap', act as a fingerprint identifying the state's class.
